@@ -157,7 +157,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

\`\`\`rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
\`\`\`

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, warn, instrument};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        command_type = ?cmd_type,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
\`\`\`

### Structured Logging Fields
\`\`\`rust
struct CommandContext {
    command_id: u64,
    command_type: &'static str,
    batch_id: Option<u64>,
}

// Log with context
debug!(
    target: "bubbletea",
    command_id = ctx.command_id,
    command_type = ctx.command_type,
    batch_id = ?ctx.batch_id,
    "Command result dropped"
);
\`\`\`

### Log Levels
- **trace**: All channel sends (very verbose, for deep debugging)
- **debug**: Failed sends during normal operation
- **warn**: Failed sends that indicate unexpected state

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing feature flag (already present)
2. Create helper function for logged send
3. Replace all \`let _ = tx.send()\` with logged version
4. Add command context tracking where feasible
5. Add documentation example
6. Add integration test for log capture

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
use tracing_test::traced_test;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn channel_disconnection_is_logged() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result dropped"));
    assert!(logs_contain("test_command"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn successful_send_logs_at_trace_level() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Trace level logs successful sends
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result sent"));
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Test
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn logs_include_command_context() {
    use tracing_subscriber::layer::SubscriberExt;
    
    let (captured, guard) = setup_log_capture();
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel);
    let cmd = Command::new(|| {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
        Some(Message::new("delayed"))
    });
    
    program.send(cmd);
    program.quit();  // Quit before command completes
    
    // Command result should be logged as dropped
    let logs = captured.into_inner();
    assert!(logs.iter().any(|l| l.contains("dropped")));
}
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: bubbletea_logging_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== bubbletea Channel Logging Tests ==="

# Enable trace logging
export RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug

# Run tests with log capture
cargo test --package bubbletea channel_disconnection -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log

# Verify expected log messages appear
if grep -q "Command result dropped" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Channel disconnection logging works"
else
    echo "[FAIL] Channel disconnection not logged"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify structured fields present
if grep -q "command_type" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Structured logging fields present"
else
    echo "[WARN] Missing structured fields - check log format"
fi

echo "=== Test Complete ==="
\`\`\`

## Documentation Example
\`\`\`rust
//! # Debugging bubbletea Command Execution
//!
//! Enable debug logging to see command execution flow:
//!
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! For even more detail:
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=trace cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! ## Log Message Reference
//!
//! | Level | Message | Meaning |
//! |-------|---------|---------|
//! | trace | "Command result sent" | Normal operation |
//! | debug | "Command result dropped" | Receiver disconnected |
//! | warn | "Command failed during active loop" | Unexpected |
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All channel send failures logged at debug level
- [ ] Logs include command context (type, id)
- [ ] Structured logging with tracing
- [ ] No performance impact when logging disabled
- [ ] Unit tests with tracing_test
- [ ] Integration test for log capture
- [ ] E2E test script validates logging
- [ ] Documentation example added","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.262130512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:33:29.920025821Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","error-handling","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d69","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.289804229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d87","title":"Docs: syntax highlighting + line numbers toggle","description":"Add syntax highlighting and optional line numbers to rendered Markdown in the Docs page.

Why:
- Syntax highlighting is a flagship Glamour feature and a major "wow" factor.
- It also demonstrates that the demo respects runtime toggles and terminal capabilities.

Requirements:
- Highlight fenced code blocks when the feature is enabled.
- Optional line numbers (toggle-able):
  - consistent width/padding
  - do not break wrapping
- Must respect:
  - no-color / ASCII mode (disable highlighting cleanly)
  - Config/Settings toggles (syntax on/off, line numbers on/off)

Performance:
- Avoid re-highlighting on every keystroke; only re-render on relevant changes (doc switch, resize, theme/toggles).

Acceptance:
- Code blocks look great and readable.
- Toggling syntax/line numbers is immediate and stable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:45.651163291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:29:17.626856556Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","glamour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d87","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:45.667314519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d87","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:36.620689828Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ecr","title":"E2E scenario: settings toggles + theme switching","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that validates Settings actually changes the running app.

Scenario outline (headless/custom-IO):
1) Launch demo_showcase in a deterministic mode (fixed seed, animations off).
2) Navigate to Settings.
3) Toggle at least:
   - no-color / ASCII mode (or equivalent)
   - mouse enable (even if headless, ensure toggle state updates)
4) Switch theme preset and confirm global restyling takes effect.
5) Return to Dashboard (or another page) and confirm the changes persist across navigation.

Assertions:
- Output changes in expected ways (e.g., theme name label updates, ANSI sequences absent when no-color).
- No panics and no hangs.

Acceptance:
- Scenario runs deterministically in CI.
- On failure, the artifact bundle clearly shows the last page and the toggle/theme state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PurpleCastle","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.112857002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:12:39.341588186Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.145700883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.141257332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150092478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.137009755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.131740545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3eru","title":"Unit tests: filtering engines (jobs/logs/docs)","description":"Add focused unit tests for filtering/search logic (without spinning up the full UI).

Why:
- Filtering and search are easy to subtly break (case sensitivity, tokenization, pagination interactions).
- We want fast feedback during refactors.

Coverage:
- Jobs filtering/sorting:
  - query matches by name/id/service
  - status filters compose correctly
  - sorting is stable and deterministic
- Logs filtering:
  - level/service selectors work
  - free-text query matches expected fields
- Docs search:
  - match finding and next/prev navigation over a rendered buffer

Edge cases to include:
- empty query
- Unicode input
- very long query (no panic, bounded behavior)
- paste-like input containing newlines/whitespace

Nice-to-have:
- Add a small set of property tests (e.g., filter is idempotent, sorting preserves multiset) if it stays lightweight.

Acceptance:
- Core filter logic is correct and stays correct during refactors.
- Tests are deterministic and run fast.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.169792772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:21:25.970193803Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-1fxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:26.759858058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-212j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:25.449597433Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:28.296789159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.182594415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3gt6","title":"Quality: README section for demo_showcase","description":"Document how to run (and trust) the showcase.

Include:
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` basics
- flags: theme/seed/no-animations/no-color/no-mouse/no-alt-screen, ssh mode, self-check
- keybindings summary + discoverability notes (help overlay, command palette if present)
- E2E instructions:
  - how to run the smoke tour and where artifacts/logs are written
  - how to run a single scenario for debugging

Also include a *feature coverage matrix* (self-contained):
- For each crate feature we claim (bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/wish/charmed_log/harmonica), list:
  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:
- Someone can run the demo and understand it without reading code.
- Someone can validate "everything works" without rediscovering the plan.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.297365816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:00.527452079Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:31.956437980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1knu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:34.482189415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.313191791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3if6","title":"Implement wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit)","description":"Implement Wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit) matching Go wish.

Scope:
- Define middleware trait/type in crates/wish to compose handlers.
- Implement standard middleware: logging, recover/panic handling, rate limiting.
- Ensure middleware order and error propagation match Go semantics.
- Add unit tests and integration tests using wish examples.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Middleware API supports chaining and is used by default server builder.
- Logging/recover/ratelimit middleware behave as expected with tests.
- Integration test demonstrates middleware in a sample server.","notes":"Files: crates/wish/src/lib.rs (or middleware module), crates/wish/examples/*. Use legacy_wish for reference.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:28.687094198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493720286Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493694748Z","close_reason":"Middleware pattern already fully implemented: logging, recover, ratelimiter, accesscontrol, activeterm, elapsed, comment - all matching Go wish","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3j9x","title":"demo_showcase: core TUI shell + router (bubbletea)","description":"Implement the reusable application shell that everything else plugs into.

Key ideas:
- One top-level App model that owns routing + global concerns (theme, status, notifications).
- Pages are sub-models/components; App delegates update/view and composes commands.
- Strong separation between domain data (fake backend) and presentation.

Acceptance:
- App boots into a multi-pane layout with navigation and at least one page.
- Clean message routing (no spaghetti downcasts everywhere).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.314919933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333853721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j9x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333820659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
